title: The Book of the Cave of Treasures
author: E. A. Wallis Budge (translator); Syriac original traditionally attributed to St. Ephrem the Syrian (Pseudo-Ephrem in modern scholarship)
edition: Translation from the Syriac text of British Museum MS. Add. 25875 (a 1709 Nestorian-hand copy by the priest Homo, son of Daniel, of Al-Kosh). Religious Tract Society, London, 1927.
sourcePdfPublisher: Global Grey ebooks (2018 republication of the 1927 edition)
originalDate: Compiled in present form c. 6th century (Syriac tradition attributes composition to St. Ephrem the Syrian, d. 373; modern scholarship dates the redaction to the 6th century, hence the conventional 'Pseudo-Ephrem' designation)
copyrightNote: Budge's 1927 translation is public domain in the US (works published before 1929 are PD). Budge died in 1934, placing the work in PD throughout the EU (life+70) as of 2005. The Global Grey 2018 typesetting/PDF is a faithful reproduction of the 1927 text and adds no new copyrightable material.
structure: 5 sections of 'Thousand Years' covering Creation to Christ's birth (chapters 1-5), one section covering 'The Five Hundred Years from the Second Year of Cyrus to the Birth of Christ' (chapter 6), and one appendix 'Testamentum Adami' (chapter 7). Budge's Preface, Introduction, supplementary translations from the Book of the Bee, his essay on Abraham and Ur, illustrations index, and bibliography are excluded — they are Budge's editorial apparatus, not the Cave of Treasures itself.
